entries.summary: President Biden signed the CHIPS and Science Act, providing 52 billion dollars for semiconductors.
Nvidia disclosed US restrictions on its A100 and H100 AI chip sales to China.
Commerce Department announced sweeping export controls limiting US chip technology to China.
South Korea's SK hynix and Samsung received one-year waivers from the new chip rules.
Commerce Department added Yangtze Memory and other Chinese firms to the Entity List.
Reports said Japan and the Netherlands would join US chip-export curbs on China.
Netherlands announced new export restrictions on advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment.
China's cyberspace regulator banned key infrastructure operators from buying Micron chips.
Huawei launched the Mate 60 Pro using a Chinese-made advanced chip during Raimondo's visit.
Commerce Department tightened the October 2022 controls, closing chip-export loopholes.
Updated ASML restrictions on advanced lithography exports to China took effect.
Commerce Department further tightened the chip export controls, expanding licensing requirements.
Intel said US chip-export rules forced revised Gaudi 3 plans for Chinese customers.
Commerce revoked Huawei licences held by Intel and Qualcomm for selling chips.
Commerce proposed export restrictions on quantum computing and advanced chip-making tools.
Commerce expanded controls, adding 140 Chinese firms to the chip-related Entity List.
Biden administration launched a Section 301 investigation into Chinese legacy chip production.
Biden signed the CHIPS Act, aimed at boosting domestic semiconductor production.
Nvidia said new US export rules barred selling its top AI chips to China.
Washington unveiled sweeping curbs on chip and equipment exports to China.
United States blacklisted Chinese chipmaker YMTC and dozens of other firms.
Japan and Netherlands reportedly agreed to join US chip export controls on China.
Netherlands set out new restrictions on exporting advanced chip-making machines.
China barred operators of key information infrastructure from purchasing US firm Micron's products.
China imposed export controls on gallium and germanium, both used in semiconductor production.
Huawei released a 5G-capable smartphone using domestically produced advanced chip technology.
United States updated October 2022 rules, closing loopholes around AI chip exports.
Washington tightened chip-export rules, broadening firms required to obtain licences.
United States revoked Intel and Qualcomm licences to sell chips to Huawei.
Washington proposed export controls on quantum computers and advanced chip-making tools.
United States added 140 Chinese chip firms to its restricted entity list.
China retaliated, banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium and antimony.
Biden launched a trade probe into China's legacy semiconductor manufacturing practices.
